req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 3
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement enforces a 7-day minimum scheduling horizon and mandates a webhook emission on success. Four test cases cover: (1) EP-positive — a valid date well inside the allowed window; (2) NEG-negative — a date in the past; (3) BVA-boundary — exactly 7 days from now (inclusive lower bound, must succeed); (4) BVA-boundary — exactly 6 days 23 hours 59 minutes from now (one minute short, must fail with 422). The webhook assertion is included in the positive case to cover the 'additional variant semantics' requirement. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and uses freeze-time or explicit datetime arithmetic to remain deterministic.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_succeeds_and_emits_webhook
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token> fixture
Webhook receiver stub running and accessible via <webhook_stub> fixture
Target date is now + 14 days (well inside the valid partition)
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and computed date
Poll webhook stub for emitted event
expected: Response body contains review id and status='scheduled'
Webhook stub received exactly one event with type='performance_review.scheduled'
Participants notified field is true in response
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_date_succeeds_and_emits_webhook(
    manager_auth_token, api_client, webhook_stub
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)
    ).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": "<fixture:reviewee_id>",
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert "id" in body
    assert body.get("participants_notified") is True

    events = webhook_stub.received_events(event_type="performance_review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 1
    assert events[0]["review_id"] == body["id"]

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token> fixture
Target date is now - 1 day (past partition)
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and past date
trace: R-WEB-094: past dates shall be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_returns_422(
    manager_auth_token, api_client, webhook_stub
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)
    ).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": "<fixture:reviewee_id>",
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    field_errors = [e.get("field") for e in body.get("errors", [])]
    assert "scheduled_at" in field_errors

    events = webhook_stub.received_events(event_type="performance_review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_ahead_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token> fixture
Target date is exactly utcnow + 7 days (lower valid boundary, inclusive)
actions: Compute scheduled_at = utcnow + exactly 7 days (to the second)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and boundary date
trace: R-WEB-094: at least 7 days in advance — inclusive lower bound must succeed

tests.name: test_schedule_review_one_minute_short_of_7_days_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token> fixture
Target date is utcnow + 7 days - 1 minute (one step below the valid boundary)
actions: Compute scheduled_at = utcnow + 7 days minus 1 minute
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and sub-boundary date
trace: R-WEB-094: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-094: BVA on the 7-day threshold — just below boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_one_minute_short_of_7_days_returns_422(
    manager_auth_token, api_client, webhook_stub
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7) - timedelta(minutes=1)
    ).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": "<fixture:reviewee_id>",
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    field_errors = [e.get("field") for e in body.get("errors", [])]
    assert "scheduled_at" in field_errors

    events = webhook_stub.received_events(event_type="performance_review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_schedule_review_non_manager_role_is_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated employee (non-manager) session token available via <employee_auth_token> fixture
trace: R-WEB-094: Actors — manager only; role:manager precondition
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_non_manager_role_is_rejected(
    employee_auth_token, api_client, webhook_stub
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)
    ).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": "<fixture:reviewee_id>",
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_auth_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "permission" in body.get("message", "").lower() or \
           body.get("error") == "forbidden"

    events = webhook_stub.received_events(event_type="performance_review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 0
